sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.86 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Does every group$G= < x_1, ,x_n r_1= =r_m=1 >$possess, in a natural way, a homomorphic image$H= < x_1, ,x_n s_1= =s_m=1 >$[r]{a)} such that$H$is a torsion-free group? [r]{b)} such that the integral group ring of$H$is embeddable in a skew field? If the stronger assertion ``b'' is true, then this will give an explicit method of finding elements$x_{i_1}, ,x_{i_{n-m}}$which generate a free group in$G$...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
